perturbation.original_text: Bach Party hereto agrees that this Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.changed_text: Bach Party hereto agrees that this Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process', from the original text, the modified text implies that there are absolutely no exceptions to the Proprietary Information remaining confidential. This contradicts Section 4, which discusses scenarios where Proprietary Information is requested under legal processes like subpoenas. Therefore creating a conflict, as a party is now required to disclose Proprietary Information per law, but under this modification, there are no exceptions to disclosing Proprietary Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a Party or anyone to whom a Party supplied Proprietary Information receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Proprietary Information under the terms of a subpoena, order, civil investigative demand or similar process issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by a government body, that Party shall immediately notify the other Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, consult with the other Party on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or remove such request, and if disclosure of such information is required, the Party will (a) furnish only that portion of the Proprietary Information which in its reasonable opinion, based upon advice of counsel, it is legally compelled to disclose, and (b) at the other Party's cost, cooperate with the efforts of the Party to obtain order or other reliable assurance that confidential treat will he accorded to such portion of the Proprietary Information as may be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allowed for disclosure of Proprietary Information as required by law or legal process. The changed text removes this exception, creating a scenario where no exceptions to confidentiality exist. The contradicted text (Section 4) details the procedure for disclosing Proprietary Information under subpoena or similar legal process. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts Section 4 by forbidding what Section 4 explicitly addresses, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement (except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the seine confidential) without the prior written consent of the other Patty, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Patty, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the seine confidential)', the modified text implies that legal and accounting advisors are no longer exempt from confidentiality, even with a written agreement. This modification directly contradicts established practices where such advisors typically require access to confidential information to properly advise their clients. Section 4, which speaks to legal obligations, is now contradicted. The agreement in it's original text has exceptions to the keeping of confidential information, if someone is under legal process, and with legal/accounting advisors; by removing this, it makes any exchange with a third party illegal, as the information now must remain confidential between the two parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a Party or anyone to whom a Party supplied Proprietary Information receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Proprietary Information under the terms of a subpoena, order, civil investigative demand or similar process issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by a government body, that Party shall immediately notify the other Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, consult with the other Party on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or remove such request, and if disclosure of such information is required, the Party will (a) furnish only that portion of the Proprietary Information which in its reasonable opinion, based upon advice of counsel, it is legally compelled to disclose, and (b) at the other Party's cost, cooperate with the efforts of the Party to obtain order or other reliable assurance that confidential treat will he accorded to such portion of the Proprietary Information as may be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allowed disclosure to legal and accounting advisors with a confidentiality agreement. The changed text removes this exception, meaning any disclosure, even to advisors, is now a breach. The contradicted text outlines the process for disclosing information under legal compulsion (e.g., subpoena). The changed text now makes ANY disclosure, including those compelled by law and handled as per the contradicted text, a violation of the agreement. This directly contradicts the procedure outlined in the contradicted text, which allows for limited disclosure under legal duress, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the changed text doesn't allow for ANY disclosure outside of the two parties.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Inform on shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information or its Affiliates; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party; or (c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Inform on shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information or its Affiliates; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information.', the conditions under which the Proprietary Information can be used are narrowed. By not accounting for receiving information with a believed source, the text contradicts Section 5. Section 5 mentions that the delivering party makes no representation or warranty, therefore if a party were to receive information from a believed source, there is no warranty that the information is correct.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that although the Party delivering the Proprietary Information has endeavored to include Proprietary Information that is relevant for purposes of the other Party's evaluation of a Transaction, the delivering Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Information neither the delivering Party nor its Affiliates shall have any liability to the receiving Party OR an Affiliate arising out of the riving Patty's or an Affiliate's use of the Proprietary Information or reliance thereon.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided exceptions to what constitutes Proprietary Information, including information received from a source believed to be unconstrained by confidentiality. The changed text removes this exception. The contradicted text states that the delivering party makes no warranty regarding the accuracy of the information. By removing the exception in the changed text and narrowing the definition of Proprietary Information, the receiving party could be held liable for using information received from a source they believed was unconstrained, even if that information proves inaccurate because there's no warranty on it, which directly contradicts the spirit of the original agreement where such information would not be considered Proprietary. Thus, the omission creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that preliminary dealings and discussions, and this letter, shall not be construed in any way as an obligation of either Party to go forward with or consummate any Transaction. In the event the Parties consummate any Transaction a formal and binding Agreement shall be executed by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that preliminary dealings and discussions, and this letter, shall not be construed in any way as an obligation of either Party to go forward with or consummate any Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'In the event the Parties consummate any Transaction a formal and binding Agreement shall be executed by the Parties.' from the end of the text, the implication is that there is no formal agreement required for a transaction. Therefore, after preliminary dealings and discussions, the Proprietary Information is being used without a formal process, contradicting Section 11, in which states, no effort shall be made to circumvent its terms. With no formal agreement in place, the parties could be circumventing each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties of THIS Agreement acknowledge that no effort shall be made to circumvent its terms in an attempt to gain cessions, fees, remuneration or considerations to the benefit of any of the Parties of this Agreement while excluding equal or agreed to benefits to any of the other parties. And that further, throughout the terns OF this agreement the parties agree that they shall not, as either owner, co-owner, partner, joint venture, employees, agent, sales person, service person, officer, director, stockholder, or in any other capacity whatsoever, or on behalf of any other business entity, become engaged directly or indirectly with any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement without the direct participation and authority of-the introducing party. The parties agree that any such breech of this provision shall cause severe damage for which the injured party will have the right to consequential monetary damages and injunctive relief and protection against the offending party and any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement with whom the offending party enters into such circumvention.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that if a transaction is consummated, a formal agreement would be executed. The changed text removes that requirement, implying no formal agreement is necessary. The contradicted text states that the parties must not circumvent 'THIS Agreement'. Since the changed text implies that a transaction can occur without a formal agreement, it creates a situation where parties could circumvent the requirement of a formal agreement, which contradicts the obligation not to circumvent the agreement in the contradicted text. Therefore there is a direct contradiction, which adheres to the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties of THIS Agreement acknowledge that no effort shall be made to circumvent its terms in an attempt to gain cessions, fees, remuneration or considerations to the benefit of any of the Parties of this Agreement while excluding equal or agreed to benefits to any of the other parties. And that further, throughout the terns OF this agreement the parties agree that they shall not, as either owner, co-owner, partner, joint venture, employees, agent, sales person, service person, officer, director, stockholder, or in any other capacity whatsoever, or on behalf of any other business entity, become engaged directly or indirectly with any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement without the direct participation and authority of-the introducing party. The parties agree that any such breech of this provision shall cause severe damage for which the injured party will have the right to consequential monetary damages and injunctive relief and protection against the offending party and any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement with whom the offending party enters into such circumvention.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties of THIS Agreement acknowledge that no effort shall be made to circumvent its terms in an attempt to gain cessions, fees, remuneration or considerations to the benefit of any of the Parties of this Agreement while excluding equal or agreed to benefits to any of the other parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'And that further, throughout the terns OF this agreement the parties agree that they shall not, as either owner, co-owner, partner, joint venture, employees, agent, sales person, service person, officer, director, stockholder, or in any other capacity whatsoever, or on behalf of any other business entity, become engaged directly or indirectly with any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement without the direct participation and authority of-the introducing party. The parties agree that any such breech of this provision shall cause severe damage for which the injured party will have the right to consequential monetary damages and injunctive relief and protection against the offending party and any person, firm, corporation, association, or other entity or entities introduced as a result of this agreement with whom the offending party enters into such circumvention.', the clause specifically restricting parties from engaging with introduced entities without the introducing party's consent is removed. This directly contradicts the earlier statement about not circumventing the agreement, as parties are now free to potentially bypass the introducing party, going against Section 7, which discusses remedies and damages from a branch of contract. If a breech was to occur, it would have no effect, as the section referencing 'monetary damages and injunctive relief and protection against the offending party' has been removed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands That the Proprietary Information disclosed to it is a unique and valuable asset of the other Party, that violation of this Agreement would cause the other Party immediate and irreparable harm, and that the remedies at law may be inadequate. Each Party therefore consents to the entry of a restraining order and a preliminary and permanent injunction restraining any such violation without proof of actual damages. Each Party agrees that any money damages shall include all pecuniary beets obtained by the receiving Party and its Affiliates as a result of any brunch of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a clause preventing parties from engaging with introduced entities without the introducer's consent, with remedies for breach. The changed text removes this clause, which now allows parties to bypass the introducing party, thus directly contradicting the contradicted text, which allows Each Party to obtain a restraining order and a preliminary and permanent injunction and obtain 'all pecuniary beets obtained by the receiving Party and its Affiliates as a result of any brunch of this Agreement.' This contradiction aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed, contradicting the remedies stated in the contradicted text.
